# THRIVE I — Integrated campaigns and operations

All powers install **Available Inactive**. A dashboard launcher opens a preview only; it never performs an external action.

## PWR-13 — Integrated Campaign Strategy & Human Approval Canvas

**Pillar:** I<br>
**Install state:** `Available Inactive`<br>
**Purpose:** Turn one approved growth question into a coordinated, evidence-led campaign proposal across owned, earned and partner channels.<br>
**Allowed inputs:** approved objective; aggregate audience insight; service capacity; approved claims; channels; budget ceiling; owners; measurement window<br>
**Method:** run CHART and ALIGN; define non-AI alternative and status quo; map message, channel, journey and capacity dependencies; set outcome/balancing measures and stop rules<br>
**Output:** Campaign Canvas, channel roles, content requirements, measurement plan and release queue<br>
**Human checkpoint:** named campaign, service, finance, legal and clinical owners approve launch<br>
**Prohibited:** no campaign launch, ad placement, spend, send, publication, pricing, booking, lead creation or capacity promise<br>
**Finish state:** preview receipt with source, owner, version, unresolved questions, human decision, expiry and safe exit; no external or official-system action.

## PWR-14 — Editorial, Content Operations & Review-Orchestration Board

**Pillar:** I<br>
**Install state:** `Available Inactive`<br>
**Purpose:** Make content requests, evidence, review status, expiry, reuse and retirement visible without publishing.<br>
**Allowed inputs:** approved briefs; asset inventory; claim/source links; channels; accessibility needs; owners; reviewers; expiry and retention rules<br>
**Method:** normalize asset IDs; separate draft/approved/expired; link claims to evidence; sequence human reviews; check channel adaptation and version drift; prepare retirement queue<br>
**Output:** Editorial Board, review queue, evidence links, reuse map and retirement list<br>
**Human checkpoint:** named authors and reviewers record approval in the official content system<br>
**Prohibited:** no publication, external send, approval fabrication, stale-asset reuse, rights assumption, deletion or official-record write<br>
**Finish state:** preview receipt with source, owner, version, unresolved questions, human decision, expiry and safe exit; no external or official-system action.

## PWR-15 — Channel Experiment, Measurement & Learning Lab

**Pillar:** I<br>
**Install state:** `Available Inactive`<br>
**Purpose:** Prepare a bounded, reversible learning test with meaningful outcomes and guardrails instead of vanity metrics.<br>
**Allowed inputs:** approved hypothesis; aggregate baseline; channel; variants; sample/period assumptions; outcome and balancing measures; owner; stop rule<br>
**Method:** define decision supported; compare status quo; pre-register hypothesis and exclusions; check capacity, equity and privacy; prepare analysis and human interpretation steps<br>
**Output:** Experiment Card, measure dictionary, pre-analysis plan, guardrails and decision receipt shell<br>
**Human checkpoint:** qualified marketing/data owner approves design and interprets results<br>
**Prohibited:** no experiment launch, tracking deployment, cookie/pixel addition, spend, automatic winner, individual profiling or causal overclaim<br>
**Finish state:** preview receipt with source, owner, version, unresolved questions, human decision, expiry and safe exit; no external or official-system action.

## PWR-16 — Demand-to-Service, Team Capacity, SOP & Handoff Navigator

**Pillar:** I<br>
**Install state:** `Available Inactive`<br>
**Purpose:** Align proposed marketing demand with aggregate service and team capacity, role clarity, review queues, continuity and official handoffs without touching consumer, clinical or employee-level workflows.<br>
**Allowed inputs:** approved aggregate demand/workload/capacity measures; service definitions; role/RACI; approved SOP references; review queues; owners; thresholds; official-system boundaries; continuity plan<br>
**Method:** verify numerator/denominator and freshness; model aggregate ranges; map inquiry-to-service and content-review handoffs; clarify retain/delegate/consult/escalate roles; identify SOP, review-load, continuity, accessibility and capacity questions; prepare pace, pause, delegate or redirect options<br>
**Output:** Capacity Alignment Brief, aggregate review-load view, role/RACI and delegation proposal, SOP question log, handoff map, continuity options and owner queue<br>
**Human checkpoint:** service, marketing, team, operations, HR and clinical owners validate their domains and decide changes<br>
**Prohibited:** no individual lead or employee data; monitoring, scoring, scheduling, assignment, performance or employment action; no triage, eligibility, referral, booking, waitlist, routing, safe-capacity claim or automated message<br>
**Finish state:** preview receipt with source, owner, version, unresolved questions, human decision, expiry and safe exit; no external or official-system action.
